# Handoff — VW Widget Bridge Phase B+1 (session 2026-06-19)

**Written:** end of session that delivered Phase B+1.A/B/C (bridge v0.8.2 through v0.8.5).
**For:** the next session, picking up after the VW image's input dispatch broke and the bridge was committed but v0.8.5 was not yet validated end-to-end.

## Goal

Recover the VW image from a broken input-dispatch state (likely full image restart), re-file the 7 bridge `.st` files in order, then validate v0.8.5 cascade-aware `/dialogs/respond` end-to-end against the Party Search modal before resuming `EXPLORATION-PLAN.md` steps 3 and 4.

---

## Work completed

- Verified bridge state at start (v0.8.1, token `3959264895330-504063`) and ran EXPLORATION-PLAN Step 1: Party Search widget surface enumerated via `/windows/tree` — 26 widgets including `searchCriteriaString` input, `findID` Search button, 7 radio criteria buttons, and `exactMatchID` checkbox.
- Ran EXPLORATION-PLAN Step 2 with `PP0` + `exactMatch=false`; `/click findID` wedged the bridge because the Search button popped a confirm modal that blocked the UI process, and v0.8.1's `listenerLoop` served requests inline so the whole bridge stalled.
- Diagnosed the wedge by reading [`VWBridge.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge.st), [`VWBridge-phaseA.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseA.st), [`VWBridge-phaseB.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseB.st): `serve:` ran on the single listener process, `onUIDo:` used `uiProc interruptWith:` that hung indefinitely on the modal's nested event loop, and even `/health` queued behind it.
- Wrote and filed [`VWBridge-phaseB1.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseB1.st) (v0.8.2) which forks `serve:` per connection at `userBackgroundPriority`. Verified empirically: `/health` 85ms, `/windows` 66ms, `/windows/tree` 116ms, `/value` 76ms — ALL responded during an active `/click` wedge. Patch also auto-writes the new bearer token to `src/vw-bridge/.token` so the agent picks it up without copy-paste.
- Wrote and filed [`VWBridge-phaseB2.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseB2.st) (v0.8.3) adding `POST /eval` (Smalltalk source in body, `printString` of result in response). **This was the breakthrough tool** — eliminated workspace file-in cycles for all subsequent introspection.
- Used `/eval` to discover the full Dialog mechanism: `Dialog` class is a static facade (zero instance methods, only `parentVisual` instVar), `confirm:` delegates through `confirm:for:` → `confirm:initialAnswer:for:` → `choose:labels:values:default:for:` which calls `self dialogSupplier choose:...` where `dialogSupplier` returns a `SimpleDialog` instance.
- Discovered `Dialog usesNativeDialogs` was `true` by default — VW was using native Win32 dialogs invisible to VW introspection. Set `Dialog useNativeDialogs: false` via `/eval`; this made SimpleDialog modals visible in `scheduledControllers` as `ScheduledWindow`s with widgets `YesButton` / `NoButton` / `displayMessageString`.
- Wrote and filed [`VWBridge-phaseB3.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseB3.st) (v0.8.4) adding `GET /dialogs` + `POST /dialogs/respond` and a defensive `valuesDo:` fix in `resolveAspect:` (some VW Dictionary subtypes don't implement `valuesDo:`, notably `WealthPublishedPundleVersionsTool`'s `namedComponents`, which crashed `/click` on iterations where that window came first).
- E2E-tested v0.8.4 and found bugs: `/dialogs` reported stale `SimpleDialog allInstances` as dialogs (should filter via `scheduledControllers`), `/dialogs/respond` went through `/click` but setting ValueHolders from a foreign process didn't wake the wedged modal loop, and message extraction errored because `displayMessageString`'s `spec.label` is a `ComposedText` not a `String`.
- Discovered via experiment that **`target closeAndUnschedule` on the live SimpleDialog ScheduledController** is the only reliable dismiss mechanism (other methods like `target close`, `view close`, `interruptWith:` on `activeControllerProcess` all failed).
- Wrote [`VWBridge-phaseB4.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseB4.st) (v0.8.5) which rewrites `doRespondDialog:` to use `closeAndUnschedule`, filters `doListDialogs` via `scheduledControllers`, and extracts message text via `ComposedText asString`. **NOT yet filed in or tested end-to-end.**
- User reported cascade-modal behavior: clicking No on the first modal triggers a second modal with OK that must also be dismissed. Documented but not implemented as auto-cascade (single-shot respond, caller orchestrates).
- VW image input dispatch broke during testing (`activeControllerProcess: nil`, 3 zombie Party Search processes, 3 `UnhandledException` instances). Tried termination + various recovery selectors — none worked. `ScheduledControllers` exposes only `initialize` and `resetViews` as recovery hooks. Recommended image restart.
- Created [`tm-context/knowledge/`](./README.md) with 6 cross-referenced markdown files (README, vw-dialogs, vw-bridge-testing, vw-eval-cookbook, vw-input-recovery, smalltalk-gotchas) capturing all of the above as durable knowledge.
- Made 2 clean git commits (after one failed attempt with a BOM/wrong-commit-grouping that I reset): `0d7196c feat for bridge patches + probes + .gitignore`, and `2af5339 docs for knowledge folder`. **Branch is 2 ahead of `origin/main`, not pushed per house rules.**

- **VW image is in a broken input-dispatch state.** `activeControllerProcess` is nil. User reports inability to click on VW windows. Bridge `/eval` still works (the HTTP listener is on its own process); only the UI process is wedged.
- Bridge in-memory is v0.8.4 (token `3959281272919-403070`). `Dialog useNativeDialogs: false`. `SimpleDialog allInstances = 1` (dialogSupplier singleton only — no live modal at last check). `scheduledControllers count = 6` (no SimpleDialog scheduled).
- v0.8.5 patch ([`VWBridge-phaseB4.st`](../src/vw-bridge/VWBridge-phaseB4.st)) is on disk but **NOT filed in** (user could not file in due to input lock).

- Git: branch `main`, 2 commits ahead of `origin/main` (`0d7196c`, `2af5339`), working tree clean.
- VW process is PID 5912 (`vwnt.exe`); 6 normal `OWST2` windows visible to OS, no native Win32 dialog `#32770` anywhere.

## Pending tasks

1. **Recover VW input** (likely full image restart — kill `vwnt.exe` PID 5912 in Task Manager, relaunch normally). See [`vw-input-recovery.md`](./vw-input-recovery.md) Level 4 for procedure.
2. **After image restart:** re-file all 7 bridge files in order (VWBridge.st through VWBridge-phaseB4.st — the one-liner is in [`vw-bridge-testing.md`](./vw-bridge-testing.md)).
3. **Re-toggle** `Dialog useNativeDialogs: false` via `/eval` (not persisted across image restart).
4. **Validate v0.8.5 cascade-modal flow end-to-end:**
   - Trigger `/click findID`
   - `GET /dialogs` (expect "This search can take a while. Continue?" message + Yes/No buttons)
   - `POST /dialogs/respond {"choice":"No"}`
   - `GET /dialogs` again (expect "Search cancelled" + OK)
   - `POST /dialogs/respond {"choice":"OK"}`
   - Verify `/click` background returns and `/dialogs` returns empty
5. If v0.8.5 validation succeeds: resume EXPLORATION-PLAN steps 3 (3-deep menu navigation: `Party & Contract > General reports > <leaf>`) and 4 (catalog leaf dispatch types).
6. Update [`src/vw-bridge/SESSION-RESUME.md`](../src/vw-bridge/SESSION-RESUME.md) with Phase B+1 progress (currently still describes Phase B v0.8.1).
7. Optionally push the 2 unpushed commits (don't push without explicit user OK).

## Important decisions

- Used incremental patch files (`VWBridge-phaseB1.st` through `phaseB4.st`) rather than refactoring the whole bridge — matches the existing patch-file pattern and keeps each Phase as a single auditable commit.
- `/eval` was the workflow inflection point — eliminated workspace file-in cycles, made all subsequent introspection trivial. Made the security argument that the existing bridge already accepts arbitrary file-ins, so `/eval` is not new attack surface for the 127.0.0.1-only dev workflow.
- Toggled `Dialog useNativeDialogs: false` to make modals introspectable. Did **NOT** auto-toggle this in any bridge patch — left it as user/session opt-in via `/eval` to avoid changing app behavior implicitly.
- `closeAndUnschedule` on the SimpleDialog `ScheduledController` is the canonical dismiss mechanism. Setting accept/cancel/close ValueHolders from a foreign process doesn't reliably wake the wedged modal loop (notifications fire on setter's process, not the wait process); only `closeAndUnschedule` forces an OS window-close event that the loop notices.
- `/dialogs/respond` is single-shot by design; cascade-modal handling is the caller's responsibility (call respond, check `/dialogs` again, repeat). Auto-cascade was considered but adds policy concerns (which button to auto-press) and prevents the caller from observing intermediate modals.
- Created `knowledge/` at project root (not under `src/vw-bridge/`) because the contents include VW image + Smalltalk gotchas + bridge testing that apply beyond just the bridge.
- Made 2 logical commits rather than 6 granular ones — matches the existing per-feature-or-per-version commit granularity in the repo log.
- Did **NOT** push the 2 unpushed commits and did NOT toggle `Dialog useNativeDialogs` back to true — both wait for explicit user direction.

## Explicit constraints

- From user this session: "It has to be automated remember so we have to find a way to click on the popup"
- From user this session (after wedge): "wait I can't click on the image anymore did we break something? maybe native dialogs?" — this established that breaking VW input is a real cost to avoid
- From `AGENTS.md` transaction discipline: "NEVER call `gemstone_commit` unless the user explicitly asks you to commit."
- From `AGENTS.md` style: "End every task that touched the image by running `gemstone_status` and reporting transaction state (uncommitted changes: yes/no) to the user."
- From `AGENTS.md` git/system rules: "Only commit, amend, push, or create PRs when explicitly requested."
- From `src/vw-bridge/SESSION-RESUME.md` safety rules carried forward: "These widgets remain OFF-LIMITS without explicit user permission" — `commitWidget`, `loginRpcWidget`, `removeWidget`. MAS menu leaves with mutation verbs (add/delete/transfer/commit/change/update/process/submit/upload) need explicit OK.
- From `src/vw-bridge/SESSION-RESUME.md`: "`windowTitle` is CASE-SENSITIVE — use `MOMENTUM`"
- From `src/vw-bridge/SESSION-RESUME.md`: "`$'` character literal is unreliable in `.st` file-in chunks — use `(Core.Character value: 39)` for the single-quote char"
- From `src/vw-bridge/SESSION-RESUME.md`: "GBS namespace: qualify kernel names — `Root.Smalltalk`, `Core.String`, `Core.Character`, `Core.Error`, etc."

## Context for continuation

- **The VW image is broken right now.** Before doing anything else, the user needs to restart the image and re-file all 7 `.st` files. The recovery procedure is in [`vw-input-recovery.md`](./vw-input-recovery.md).
- After image restart, `Dialog useNativeDialogs` defaults back to `true`. Re-toggle to false via `POST /eval` with body `Dialog useNativeDialogs: false` before doing any dialog work.
- The bridge auto-writes the new bearer token to `src/vw-bridge/.token` on each file-in of `VWBridge-phaseB2.st` or later. Read it with `Get-Content -LiteralPath src/vw-bridge/.token`.
- Git access requires WSL: `wsl -e git -C /mnt/c/Users/ammaganyane/tm/tm-context <command>`. PowerShell pipe to `wsl git` silently fails on multi-line commit messages — use the `.git/COMMIT_MSG_TMP` file approach with `System.IO.File.WriteAllText` + `UTF8Encoding($false)` for no BOM.
- **Cascade modal pattern:** clicking No on the search-confirm modal triggers a second "Search cancelled" modal with OK. Test runner must orchestrate via: call respond, GET `/dialogs`, repeat until empty.
- **The most important methodology lesson:** restart the bridge (re-file all `.st` files) BETWEEN major test cycles. This session's wedge happened because `/click` jobs accumulated across cycles and eventually broke VW's input dispatch. See [`vw-bridge-testing.md`](./vw-bridge-testing.md) for the discipline.
- Don't accumulate wedged `/click` jobs. If a test goes weird, restart the bridge fresh BEFORE the wedges compound.
- For all future introspection: use `POST /eval`. Don't write workspace probe `.st` files unless absolutely necessary — they require unblocked workspace input, which we now know is fragile.
- Bridge endpoints summary: `/health` (no auth), `/windows`, `/windows/tree`, `/value`, `/menu` (GET) and `/click`, `/type`, `/menu/click`, `/eval` (v0.8.3+), `/dialogs/respond` (v0.8.4+) (POST). `/value` can also be PUT.
- Stale `SimpleDialog allInstances` accumulate — always filter live dialogs via `scheduledControllers` (`ScheduledControllers scheduledControllers select: [:c | c model class == SimpleDialog]`).
- ComposedText text extraction: `aComposedText asString`. Not `displayString` (returns description), not `text` (returns wrapped form), not `string` (may include print-quotes).
- The user verified this session that the workspace IS blocked while a modal is up (system-modal, not just app-modal). `/eval` bypass works because it runs on the bridge's serve-process, not the UI process.

## To continue in a new session

1. Press `n` in OpenCode TUI to open a new session, or run `opencode` in a new terminal
2. Paste this entire file as your first message
3. Add your request: "Continue from the handoff context above. [Your next task]"

The new session will have all context needed to continue seamlessly.
